skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate still images with Google Nano Banana 2 through the RunComfy CLI. The skill documents prompt patterns, model options, resolution tiers, safety tolerance, web grounding, and when to choose related image models.
skill.summary: Generate Nano Banana 2 still images through RunComfy with practical prompt guidance.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many issues, but most backtick, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and path-traversal alerts are Markdown documentation or CLI examples. Confirmed risks are legitimate external command execution, outbound RunComfy API use, optional web grounding, generated file downloads, and local token storage. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration intent was found, so this is publishable as a medium-risk community skill with disclosure.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run the local RunComfy CLI and pass user prompts to the google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image model. This is expected functionality, but it executes an external command and depends on correct argument handling by the caller.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show CLI commands and the stated execution path. The risk is real but appears intentional for an image-generation skill rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Submission and Downloads
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts to the RunComfy model API and downloads generated results from RunComfy domains. This can expose prompt content and may fetch large generated files, although the endpoints are documented and related to the skill purpose.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names RunComfy web pages, the model API endpoint, and output download domains. The network behavior is confirmed and disclosed in the skill text.

low_findings.title: Local Token File and Output Directory Handling
low_findings.description: The skill documents local token storage under the user configuration directory and writes generated images to an output directory. This is standard CLI behavior, but users should understand that credentials and generated files touch the local filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show output directory use and token file documentation. The behavior is sensitive but described as owner-only token storage, so severity remains moderate to low.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: Several high and low static alerts are not supported by semantic review. The path traversal alert is a Markdown sibling-skill link, and the weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts occur in descriptive text, tables, or headings rather than executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found Markdown front matter, prose, tables, and a relative documentation link. No executable cryptographic logic, reconnaissance command, or filesystem traversal operation is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run the local RunComfy CLI and pass user prompts to the google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image model. This is expected functionality, but it executes an external command and depends on correct argument handling by the caller.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show CLI commands and the stated execution path. The risk is real but appears intentional for an image-generation skill rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Submission and Downloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends prompts to the RunComfy model API and downloads generated results from RunComfy domains. This can expose prompt content and may fetch large generated files, although the endpoints are documented and related to the skill purpose.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names RunComfy web pages, the model API endpoint, and output download domains. The network behavior is confirmed and disclosed in the skill text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Token File and Output Directory Handling
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents local token storage under the user configuration directory and writes generated images to an output directory. This is standard CLI behavior, but users should understand that credentials and generated files touch the local filesystem.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show output directory use and token file documentation. The behavior is sensitive but described as owner-only token storage, so severity remains moderate to low.

content.user_title: Generate Nano Banana Images on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Teams need fast image drafts that follow layout, text, and aspect-ratio requirements. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Nano Banana 2 through RunComfy with practical prompt patterns.
actual_capabilities: Routes text-to-image requests to the RunComfy Nano Banana 2 model endpoint.
Documents supported aspect ratios, output formats, resolution tiers, seeds, and safety tolerance.
Provides CLI invocation patterns for drafts, batches, final renders, and web-grounded prompts.
Gives prompt guidance for subject-first descriptions and quoted in-image typography.
Explains when to use related models for editing, portraits, stylization, or higher realism.
limitations: It creates still images only and does not support video generation.
The endpoint supports a maximum of four outputs per request.
Web search adds cost and latency and should only be enabled when needed.
Image editing is delegated to a sibling Nano Banana Edit skill.

use_cases.title: Draft Social Creative
use_cases.description: Generate several low-resolution vertical concepts before selecting one for final rendering.

use_cases.title: Create Product Cards
use_cases.description: Produce branded product visuals with quoted headline text, controlled framing, and clean studio lighting.

use_cases.title: Prototype Campaign Imagery
use_cases.description: Compare aspect ratios, seeds, and prompt variants before committing budget to high-resolution assets.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Image Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one standard image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 1K square Nano Banana 2 image of a modern coffee shop counter with soft morning light.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Concept Round
prompt_templates.scenario: Create multiple draft options for review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana 2 to create four 0.5K vertical concepts for a fitness app launch, with bold quoted text at the top.

prompt_templates.title: Seeded Final Render
prompt_templates.scenario: Refine a chosen direction with stable composition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Render the selected product-card concept at 2K in 16:9, keep seed 42, and preserve the balanced negative space.

prompt_templates.title: Web-Grounded Visual
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs current context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a web-grounded editorial image about this week's climate technology conference, using accurate venue context and clear signage.

output_examples.input: A designer asks for four vertical thumbnail concepts for a wellness brand launch.
output: The assistant selects Nano Banana 2 for fast draft iteration.
It requests four low-resolution vertical images with quoted brand text.
It saves the generated files to the chosen output directory.

output_examples.input: A marketer asks for a final 16:9 product card with stable layout and readable text.
output: The assistant locks a seed for reproducible composition.
It uses a 2K resolution tier for the final asset.
It quotes the exact in-image headline and specifies placement.

output_examples.input: A user asks for an image tied to a current public event.
output: The assistant enables web grounding because current context is needed.
It warns that the request may take longer and cost more.
It keeps the prompt focused on verifiable visual details.

best_practices: Start prompts with the main subject, then add environment, style, camera, and constraints.
Quote all in-image text exactly and describe its placement and font style.
Use low resolution for exploration, then increase resolution only for selected finals.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many conflicting visual styles in one prompt.
Do not request unsupported aspect ratios or custom resolution values.
Do not use this text-to-image skill when the task requires preserving an uploaded subject.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates still images from text prompts using Nano Banana 2 through the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it support image editing?
faq.answer: No. The skill points editing requests to a sibling Nano Banana Edit skill.

faq.question: Why does it need network access?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI submits prompts to the RunComfy model API and downloads generated image files.

faq.question: Can it make multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented endpoint supports up to four outputs per request.

faq.question: When should web grounding be enabled?
faq.answer: Enable it only when the prompt depends on current events, real entities, or fresh context.

faq.question: How should users improve text inside images?
faq.answer: They should quote the exact text and specify placement, font style, color, and background contrast.
